request.question: What was Robert Tree Cody's adopted father's most prominent PSA role?

title: Tachibana Dōsetsu
Robert Tree Cody
Bhuvanaikabahu VI of Kotte
Kujō Fusazane
Iron Eyes Cody
Problem Child 2
Shane Shamrock
Thadeus Nguyễn Văn Lý
Philippus (son of Philip V)
Sumako Matsui
sentences: Tachibana Dōsetsu (立花 道雪 , April 22, 1513 – November 2, 1585) , born Betsugi Akitsura (戸次鑑連 ) , also as Bekki Akitsura, and Bekki Dōsetsu, was a Japanese samurai of the Sengoku period who served the Ōtomo clan.
He was the father of Tachibana Ginchiyo and adopted father of Tachibana Muneshige.
Robert Tree Cody (born April 20, 1951) is a Native American musician, dancer, and educator.
He graduated from John Marshall High School in 1969.
Robert is an adopted son of Hollywood actor Iron Eyes Cody.

Bhuvanekabahu VI (Sapumal Kumaraya) (Sinhala:සපුමල් කුමාරයා, Tamil: செண்பகப் பெருமாள்) or Chempaha Perumal (died 1480) was by self admission an adopted son of Parakramabâhu VI whose principal achievement was the conquest of Jaffna Kingdom in the year 1447 or 1450.
He ruled the Kingdom for 17 years when he was apparently summoned to the south after the demise of his adopted father.

According to a primary source "Rajavaliya", he killed the grand son of Parakrama Bahu VI namely Vira Parakrama Bahu or Jaya Bahu (1468 – c. 1470) but Do Couto, however, who was well-informed, says after a few years' reign the king died and his half-witted son was put on the throne by his aunt, who two years later finding herself unable to rule sent for Sapumal Kumaraya from Jaffna.
Kujō Fusazane (九条 房実 , 1290–1327) , son of regent Tadanori and adopted son of Moronori, was a "kugyō" or Japanese court noble of the Kamakura period (1185–1333).
He held a regent position kampaku from 1323 to 1324.
A daughter of Nijō Kanemoto was one of his wives with whom he adopted Michinori, son of his adopted father Moronori.
Iron Eyes Cody (born Espera Oscar de Corti April 3, 1904 – January 4, 1999) was an Italian-American actor.
He portrayed Native Americans in Hollywood films.
He also played a Native American shedding a tear about litter in one of the country's most well-known television PSAs, "Keep America Beautiful".
In 1996, Cody's half-sister said that he was of Italian ancestry, but he denied it.

Problem Child 2 is a 1991 American comedy film and a sequel to the 1990 film "Problem Child"; a continuation of the exploits of Junior (Michael Oliver), an adopted orphan boy who deliberately wreaks comedic havoc everywhere he goes.
John Ritter returns as Junior's adopted father, Ben Healy.
Amy Yasbeck, who played Ben's wife, Flo, in the first movie, also returns, as school nurse Annie Young.
It was produced by producer Robert Simonds, who also produced the first one.
It was rated PG-13, unlike its predecessor, which was rated PG.
Brian Stephen Howser (February 8, 1975 – August 18, 1998) was an American professional wrestler best known for wrestling in Maryland Championship Wrestling.
In the promotion, he was the first and only wrestler to win the Light Heavyweight Championship and was nicknamed "The Lifetime MCW Light Heavyweight Champion" in tribute to his death.
Each year, the Shane Shamrock Memorial Cup is held as a tribute to him.
Despite sharing an in-ring last name, he is not related to Ken Shamrock (who was born Kenneth Wayne Kilpatrick, although he adopted the surname "Shamrock" after his adopted father, Bob Shamrock).

Father Thadeus Nguyễn Văn Lý (born 15 May 1946) is a Vietnamese Roman Catholic priest and dissident involved in many pro-democracy movements, for which he was imprisoned for a total of almost 15 years.
For his ongoing imprisonment and continuous non-violent protest, Amnesty International adopted Father Lý in December 1983 as a prisoner of conscience.
Most recently, his support for the Bloc 8406 manifesto has led to his sentence on 30 March 2007, for an additional eight years in prison, where he was released and then returned in 2011.
Philippus the son of Philip V, half-brother of Perseus Antigonid King of Macedon.
He was adopted by his brother as his son.
Even after the birth of the king's son, Alexander, Perseus treated him as his heir to the throne.
Together they surrendered to the Romans ending the Third Macedonian War.
He was led in triumph before the car of Lucius Aemilius Paullus and afterwards consigned to captivity at Alba Fucens, where he survived his adopted father but a short time.
He was only eighteen years old at the time of his death.
Sumako Matsui (松井 須磨子 , Matsui Sumako , November 1, 1886 – January 5, 1919) was a Japanese actress and singer.

Born as Masako Kobayashi in Matsushiro, Nagano, Nagano Prefecture as the fifth daughter and last of nine children of Tohta Kobayashi, she was adopted by the Hasegawa family in Ueda at the age of six and in 1900 graduated Ueda school.
She had to return to her birth family after her adopted father died, however in the year of her return, her natural father also died.
At the age of 17 she moved to Tokyo.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Produce a compact, structured first-hop summary that helps the next agent fetch the missing second-hop facts. The summary must be one labeled block with these four short sections (each 1–4 lines):

1) Evidence found — For each concrete target term or name mentioned in the passages, list the target and which passage(s) mention it, and quote a short verbatim excerpt (≤15 words) or any exact numeric/date token from the passage. Preserve the quoted text and any numeric/date formatting exactly as it appears in the passages. Include passage identifier(s) exactly as given (title or index).

2) Ambiguities — Enumerate any names or terms that correspond to multiple candidate pages or where the passages return multiple variants or conflicting values. For each ambiguous item, list the candidate labels or note the conflict using only tokens from the passages where possible.

3) Bridge / missing fact — State exactly which relation(s) or fact(s) needed to answer the question are not present or are unresolved in the provided passages (e.g., birth date, publication year, author of adaptation, founding year, award count). Be explicit and concise.

4) Retrieval direction — Provide 2–4 concrete next-hop retrievals: specific page titles or disambiguation pages, or precise search queries the next agent should run to fetch the missing bridge facts. Use actionable, precise phrasing (page title or quoted query). End this section with an explicit deferral of the final answer when the passages do not unambiguously supply all required facts.

Formatting and behavior rules:
- Produce only the single field `summary` as the output. The `summary` text must follow the four labeled sections above.
- Always quote verbatim evidence (names, short excerpts, dates, numbers) exactly as in the passages; do not reformat numeric/date tokens.
- Do NOT produce a final answer here unless every required fact to resolve the question is present and unambiguous in the provided passages (i.e., each named entity corresponds to a single candidate and the needed relation value is explicitly present). If facts are unambiguous and complete, you may state that and include a concise final conclusion in the `summary`; otherwise explicitly defer (for example: "Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.").
- If the natural summary would otherwise be a single bare token or name, append a one-line bridge clause that identifies the missing relation and at least one retrieval target rather than ending with the bare token.
- Keep phrasing concise and factual; favor short lines, source-linked evidence excerpts, and explicit next-step instructions. Do not attempt to fetch pages yourself—only list retrieval targets/queries.

Example structure to follow in the `summary` field (use passage text and titles exactly):
Evidence found: ...
Ambiguities: ...
Bridge / missing fact: ...
Retrieval direction: ...

End of instructions.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Required output format and constraints:
- Produce exactly one field named `query`. Its value must be a single short, keyword-style search string (no full sentences, no explanations, no assertions, no user-facing clarifications).
- The query must be terse (preferably <= 12 words) and contain no clause punctuation or sentence grammar (no question marks, no leading "who/what is", no verbs forming assertions).
- Use only tokens that are visible in `question` and/or `summary_1`, except you may insert at most one minimal neutral connector token to target bridging evidence (allowed connector tokens include, but are not limited to: artist, album, director, who directed, biography, introduction, genre, regular season). Do not invent new entity names or facts.
- When the inputs present multiple entities or candidate items that must be linked (for example two titles, two names, or an anchor title plus a candidate), include both entity tokens from the inputs and a single neutral connector token between or adjacent to them to indicate the evidence bridge to retrieve (e.g., "EntityA EntityB director").
- If the question or summary includes structured tokens relevant to the bridge (years, league IDs, edition names), include those tokens plus a concise cue (for example year + league + "regular season") as part of the short query.
- You may perform light normalization of obvious misspellings in entity names or technical terms drawn from the visible inputs (fix clear typos), but do not invent alternative candidate entities or assert corrected identities beyond the token-level fix.
- If it is not possible to form a fully disambiguating query from visible tokens, still emit the best compact retrieval query possible using only visible tokens and an allowed connector; do not ask the user for clarification at this step. (Downstream retrieval will be attempted first; user clarification is allowed only after retrieval fails.)

Produce only the `query` field value (the single short search string) and nothing else.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Instructions for producing `summary` (single text field only):
- Output exactly one concise summary string (no extra fields, labels, or metadata). Keep the summary to 1–2 short sentences unless the evidence forces a single-token identification (see below).
- Primary goal: integrate the first-hop identity in `context` with facts found in `passages` so the final answer agent has the explicit, supported linkage it needs.

When the question requires linking the context entity to information in the passages and the passages supply the bridging fact:
- Produce a concise (1–2 sentence) integrated summary that (1) names the context entity (using the form present in `context`), (2) names the supporting entity/attribute found in `passages` (using the form present in the inputs), and (3) states the explicit relation or fact (membership, creator attribution, partnership, founding/establishment, award, etc.) exactly as supported by the inputs. Use only facts present in `context` and `passages` and avoid introducing any unsupported details.

Name and attribution formatting:
- If a canonical multi-token name (given + family name or full official name) appears in the inputs, use that full form in the summary; do not truncate to a surname or partial name.
- For questions about creator/author/credit, prefer the person-name attribution as given in the inputs over returning the title of a work or event.

Identification requests:
- If the question explicitly asks for a single identification (for example, asks "which X" or otherwise requests a named token or label), return exactly the canonical name(s) as presented in the passages. If multiple valid names are requested, return them comma-separated. Do not add explanatory clauses, relational fragments, or extra text in these cases—only the canonical name(s).

When evidence is missing or ambiguous:
- If the passages do not supply a clear bridging fact, state that succinctly (e.g., "No supporting evidence in the provided passages" or "Unclear from the provided sources"). Retain uncertainty rather than asserting unsupported facts.

Conciseness and fidelity constraints:
- Keep the summary short and factual (1–2 sentences). Do not expand into long biographies, exhaustive lists, or unrelated details.
- Do not assert facts that are not present in `context` and `passages`.
- Avoid long verbatim copying of passage text, but short verbatim names or phrases necessary for fidelity are allowed.
- Treat the `context` field as the primary first-hop identity to be linked to `passages` when integrating evidence.

Produce the `summary` now based only on the supplied `question`, `context`, and `passages`.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Use summary_1 and summary_2 as the sole evidential basis for the final answer; give greatest weight to summary_2 (the later, post-retrieval integrated evidence). Do not introduce facts from outside these summaries.

Output rules (apply in order):

1) If summary_1 and summary_2 clearly and unambiguously agree on the same concise answer, set the value of `answer` to exactly one minimal string and nothing else:
   - For a binary yes/no conclusion, emit the single lowercase token: yes or no.
   - For any other concise label, emit that label exactly as it appears in the summaries (trim leading/trailing whitespace and strip any surrounding punctuation or wrapper syntax such as surrounding parentheses, quotes, or JSON-like braces). Do not add words, sentences, punctuation, quotes, brackets, or any other surrounding text.

2) Preserve multi-token labels: if any visible summary contains a multi-token form of the answer (for example a full personal name, a multi-word company or place), output that full multi-token form verbatim. Only shorten a label if no multi-token form appears in either summary.

3) Company/developer preference: when the question requests a company or developer and the summaries show a subordinate studio together with an explicit parent/publisher label (e.g., in parentheses or with phrasing like "published by"), choose the parent/publisher name as the single minimal answer string. If no parent/publisher is explicitly indicated, choose the best-supported developer/studio name from the summaries.

4) If the summaries conflict or are ambiguous, do NOT apply the unanimous-agreement rule in (1). Instead, use summary_2 as the primary basis and emit the minimal concise string supported by summary_2, applying rules (2) and (3) as relevant. If summary_2 provides no concise supported answer, set `answer` to the minimal explicit token "unknown".

Formatting constraints (must always be followed):
- Produce exactly one field: `answer`.
- The value of `answer` must be a single minimal string (no surrounding text, punctuation, explanation, or structural wrappers).
- Do not output extra fields, labels, or markup.

Additional guidance:
- If summary_1 describes an earlier unresolved bridge but summary_2 provides a resolved, supported conclusion, use the conclusion from summary_2.
- If a concise answer string can be drawn verbatim from the summaries, prefer that verbatim text over paraphrase.

End.

response.hop1_query: What was Robert Tree Cody's adopted father's most prominent PSA role?
hop1_docs: Robert Tree Cody | Robert Tree Cody (born April 20, 1951) is a Native American musician, dancer, and educator.  He graduated from John Marshall High School in 1969.  Robert is an adopted son of Hollywood actor Iron Eyes Cody.

Buffalo Bill Boyhood Home | The Buffalo Bill Boyhood Home was built by Isaac Cody, the father of Buffalo Bill Cody in 1841 at LeClaire, Iowa.  The house was purchased as a tourist attraction by the Chicago, Burlington and Quincy Railroad and was moved to Cody, Wyoming, Buffalo Bill's adopted hometown, in 1933.

Cody Estes | Cody Estes (born March 13, 1994) is an American actor.  His most recent role was as Charlie Cohen in "Crossing Jordan", but his most frequent role has been younger versions of John "J.D." Dorian on the medical comedy "Scrubs".

LGBT adoption and parenting in Australia | Family law in Australia with regard to children is often based on what is considered to be in the best interest of the child.  The traditional and often used assumption is that children need both a mother and a father, which plays an important role in divorce and custodial proceedings, and has carried over into adoption and fertility procedures.  As of February 2017 all Australian states and territories except for the Northern Territory allow adoption by same-sex couples.

The Father Clements Story | The Father Clements Story is a 1987 made-for-television movie about the life of Father George Clements, an African-American Roman Catholic priest who became famous for being the first United States priest to legally adopt a child.  The movie starred actors Lou Gossett, Jr., Malcolm-Jamal Warner, and Carroll O'Connor.  Gossett, Jr. played Clements, Warner played Clements' adopted son, and O'Connor played Cardinal John Cody, the Archbishop of Chicago.  The movie was directed by Edwin Sherin.  The film score was composed by Mark Snow.

Murder of Robert Schwartz | The murder of Robert Schwartz occurred on December 8, 2001 in Leesburg, Virginia.  The crime was orchestrated by his 20-year-old daughter, Clara Jane Schwartz, as part of a fantasy role-playing game.  Clara was convicted of first-degree murder for orchestrating her father's murder.  The case made national headlines due to Robert Schwartz's prominence in the scientific community and for claims that his murder was related to role-playing games and the occult.

Reed A. Albee | Reed Adalbert Albee (8 September 1885 – 2 August 1961) was an American businessman.  He is most noted as the adoptive father of the American playwright Edward Albee and for being a member of a prominent East Coast family who owned several theaters.
hop1_titles: LGBT adoption and parenting in Australia
response.summary_1:
Evidence found:
- Robert Tree Cody — [1] "Robert Tree Cody (born April 20, 1951)"
- Robert is adopted son of Iron Eyes Cody — [1] "Robert is an adopted son of Hollywood actor Iron Eyes Cody."
- Buffalo Bill / place "Cody" occurrences — [2] "father of Buffalo Bill Cody"; [2] "Cody, Wyoming"
- Other "Cody" personal name — [3] "Cody Estes (born March 13, 1994)"

Ambiguities:
- The token "Cody" maps to multiple candidates in the passages: "Iron Eyes Cody" ([1]); "Buffalo Bill Cody" ([2]); "Cody, Wyoming" ([2]); "Cody Estes" ([3]).

Bridge / missing fact:
- The passages do NOT state which PSA role Iron Eyes Cody is most prominently known for (e.g., the specific PSA name or role title is missing).

Retrieval direction:
- Fetch page: "Iron Eyes Cody" (Wikipedia or main bio page for Iron Eyes Cody)
- Fetch page or article: "Crying Indian" (or "The Crying Indian" ad) — or "Keep America Beautiful (public service announcement)"
- Search query: "Iron Eyes Cody Crying Indian Keep America Beautiful PSA"
- Search query: "Iron Eyes Cody best known for PSA role 'Crying Indian'"

Final answer deferred pending retrieval of the targets below.
response.hop2_query: Iron Eyes Cody Crying Indian PSA
hop2_docs: Iron Eyes Cody | Iron Eyes Cody (born Espera Oscar de Corti April 3, 1904 – January 4, 1999) was an Italian-American actor.  He portrayed Native Americans in Hollywood films.  He also played a Native American shedding a tear about litter in one of the country's most well-known television PSAs, "Keep America Beautiful".  In 1996, Cody's half-sister said that he was of Italian ancestry, but he denied it.

Robert Tree Cody | Robert Tree Cody (born April 20, 1951) is a Native American musician, dancer, and educator.  He graduated from John Marshall High School in 1969.  Robert is an adopted son of Hollywood actor Iron Eyes Cody.

Grayeagle | Grayeagle is a 1977 Western adventure film directed Charles B. Pierce, written by Brad White, Charles B. Pierce and Michael O. Sajbel, starring Ben Johnson, Iron Eyes Cody, and Lana Wood.  The music was composed by Jaime Mendoza-Nava.  The theme is about kidnapping and interracial/cross-cultural romance.

Perils of the Royal Mounted | Perils of the Royal Mounted (1942) was the 18th serial released by Columbia Pictures.  It starred Robert Kellard as the hero, Sgt. Mack MacLane of the Royal Mounties, and Kenneth MacDonald as Mort Ramsome, the head villain.  It also co-starred Nell O'Day, Iron Eyes Cody, Kermit Maynard and I. Stanford Jolley.

Chase Iron Eyes | Chase Iron Eyes is an American Indian activist, attorney, politician, and a member of the Standing Rock Sioux Tribe.  He is a member of the Lakota People's Law Project and a co-founder of the Native American news website "Last Real Indians".  In April 2016 he announced his candidacy for the United States House of Representatives for North Dakota's at-large congressional district.  He was unsuccessful.

Black Eyes (EP) | Black Eyes is the third mini album by South Korean girl group T-ara.  Its release was originally set for release on November 18, 2011, but was pushed forward one week to November 11 due to the overwhelming demand for the album's lead track, "Cry Cry".  "Cry Cry" has sold 3,755,993 digital units in South Korea.

Ernest Goes to Camp | Ernest Goes to Camp is a 1987 comedy film directed by John R. Cherry III and starring Jim Varney.  It is the second film to feature the character of Ernest P. Worrell and was shot at Montgomery Bell State Park.  It was also the first Ernest film to be distributed by Touchstone Pictures, and Iron Eyes Cody's final appearance on screen.  It is also the first film to feature the "Ernest" name in its title.
response.summary_2: Robert Tree Cody's adopted father, Iron Eyes Cody, played the Native American who sheds a tear about litter in the "Keep America Beautiful" television PSA.
response.answer: the Native American who sheds a tear about litter in the Keep America Beautiful television PSA
